annotations.description: The refactor abstracts promotional plugin-eligibility logic into a reusable useIsPluginAvailableOnAllPlans hook, updates plugin details CTA copy and pricing USPs to use generic all-paid-plans messaging while excluding marketplace and preinstalled premium plugins, modifies the upgrade/install navigation to route eligible users through the plans page with a redirect back to plugin installation, and threads an optional redirectTo parameter through the plans features infrastructure so checkout URLs preserve the plugin install destination.
Exported hook exists with the specified path and boolean return.
annotations.justification: [1: Prompt/Interface] Required for the stated refactor. [5: Conclusion] Observable in gold.patch scope.

title: 1.2: PlanUSPS uses promotional eligibility from useIsPluginAvailableOnAllPlans to switch to generic all-paid-plans messaging

annotations.description: PlanUSPS component consumes the useIsPluginAvailableOnAllPlans result (directly or via prop) to switch between generic 'all paid plans' messaging with the lowest plan's monthly cost and specific plan-tier messaging.
annotations.evidence: problem_statement.md: 'the plan USPs section shows the lowest available plan's cost as a monthly starting price with generic all paid plans wording'.
annotations.justification: [1: Prompt] The prompt requires generic all-paid-plans messaging in PlanUSPS when the promotional condition is active. [5: Conclusion] Verifiable by checking that PlanUSPS renders 'all paid plans' text when promotion is active.

title: 1.3: FreePrice shows generic 'on paid plans' label instead of a specific plan name when promotional condition is active

annotations.description: When the promotional condition is active, the FreePrice component displays 'on paid plans' text instead of referencing a specific plan name like 'on Business plan'. The promotional value may be obtained directly via useIsPluginAvailableOnAllPlans or passed as a prop.
annotations.evidence: problem_statement.md: 'the pricing labels generically reference paid plans instead of a specific plan name'.
annotations.justification: [1: Prompt] The prompt requires generic paid-plans labeling when promotion is active. [5: Conclusion] Verifiable by checking FreePrice renders 'on paid plans' instead of 'on Business plan' under promotion.

title: 1.4: CTAButton calls useIsPluginAvailableOnAllPlans for the selected site

annotations.description: CTAButton component invokes useIsPluginAvailableOnAllPlans with the selected site ID.
annotations.justification: [1: Prompt/Interface] Required for the stated refactor. [5: Conclusion] Observable in gold.patch scope.

title: 1.5: CTAButton install flow uses useIsPluginAvailableOnAllPlans to determine upgrade routing

annotations.description: CTAButton's plugin install flow consumes the useIsPluginAvailableOnAllPlans result to decide whether to route eligible users to the plans page or directly to checkout. The flag may be passed into a helper function or used inline.
annotations.evidence: problem_statement.md: 'users are redirected to the plans page to choose their preferred plan before checkout instead of going straight to checkout with a specific plan'.
annotations.justification: [1: Prompt] The prompt requires conditional routing based on promotional eligibility in the plugin install flow. [5: Conclusion] Verifiable by confirming CTAButton uses the promotional flag to alter the install/upgrade navigation.

title: 1.6: Plugin install flow routes to /plans/ with redirect_to preserving plugin install URL when promo active

annotations.description: When the promotional condition is active for a free plugin requiring upgrade, the install action navigates to /plans/ with a redirect_to parameter pointing to the plugin install URL, instead of routing directly to checkout. This may be implemented in a dedicated function or inline.
annotations.evidence: problem_statement.md: 'users are redirected to the plans page to choose their preferred plan before checkout...The plans page should then preserve a redirect destination through to checkout so that after purchase the user ends up back at the plugin installation page.'
annotations.justification: [1: Prompt] The prompt requires plans-page routing with redirect preservation for the install flow. [5: Conclusion] Verifiable by confirming the install action navigates to /plans/ with redirect_to when promotion is active.

title: 4.1: Modifies test files in the repository

annotations.description: Checks that no test files are modified, created, or deleted by the solution.
annotations.evidence: The prompt states 'I've already taken care of all changes to the test files. Do NOT modify any test files or testing logic in any way. Your task is to make the minimal changes to non-test source files only.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt explicitly forbids modifying test files: 'Do NOT modify any test files or testing logic in any way.' [5: Conclusion] Any modification to test files directly violates an explicit constraint of the prompt.

title: 4.2: Hardcodes the promotional condition instead of consuming useSummerSpecialStatus from @automattic/plans-grid-next

annotations.description: Checks that the solution delegates promotional status determination to the existing useSummerSpecialStatus hook rather than implementing custom logic to determine whether the promotion is active.
annotations.evidence: The prompt states 'The useSummerSpecialStatus hook from @automattic/plans-grid-next already determines whether this promotional condition is active' and asks to abstract it into a reusable hook 'without them needing to know about the underlying summer special implementation.'
annotations.justification: [1: Explicit Prompt Requirement] The prompt identifies useSummerSpecialStatus as the existing mechanism for determining promotional status. [3: Logical Necessity] Hardcoding the promotional condition instead of using the existing hook would duplicate logic, create maintenance burden, fall out of sync with the backend configuration. [5: Conclusion] Bypassing the existing promotional status hook is an anti-pattern that any correct solution should avoid.
